diagrams.title: Distributed System Architecture Overview
diagrams.description: Shows how multiple computers connected via network appear as single system to users
elements: User Interface Layer (appears as single system)
Multiple Nodes (each with OS, CPU, Memory, Storage)
diagrams.purpose: Understand the layered architecture and how transparency is achieved

diagrams.title: Client-Server vs Peer-to-Peer Architecture
diagrams.description: Comparison of two fundamental distributed system models
elements: Client-Server: Client nodes (request) → Central Server (processes) → Client nodes (response)
Peer-to-Peer: All nodes connected in mesh, each can request and provide resources
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the structural differences and roles of nodes in each model

diagrams.title: CAP Theorem Visualization
diagrams.description: Triangle showing trade-offs between Consistency, Availability, and Partition Tolerance
elements: Partition Tolerance vertex (always required in distributed systems)
Only two vertices can be fully achieved simultaneously
diagrams.purpose: Understand fundamental design trade-offs in distributed systems

diagrams.title: Distributed System Challenges
diagrams.description: Overview of key challenges in designing and managing distributed systems
elements: SYNCHRONIZATION: Clock differences, ordering of events
CONSISTENCY: Maintaining coherent data across nodes
diagrams.purpose: Identify and remember the major challenges faced by distributed systems
